mentions.note: One of best machinery companies in world; solid industrial diversifier.
Data center backup generator play; used to dip on earnings, now rallies because people can't believe consistent strength. Still rallying, wrong not to like it.
Generator business soaring on AI data center power demand; CAT +3.4% on earnings; stock at $665 vs. $393 year ago.
Tangible products in high demand; makes things people understand; infrastructure and industrial demand strong.
February winner; earth mover benefiting from data center infrastructure builds; CEO at K Expo discussing generator demand.

Caller bought at $332 (23 months ago), more than doubled; CEO Joe Creed executing well; no headwinds (metals solid, Permian strong, road construction ongoing); could be 'gigantically' higher if Iran war ends.

Multiple paths to win: infrastructure spending, construction, data-center generators. Can deploy thousands of Cat engines as backup power for data centers via natural-gas patches without raising electric rates.
Expect huge number; data center buildout is big driver (turbines, fuel cells for power).

Unexpected winner: investors buying hundreds of Cat engines for private off-grid power plants to power data centers with West Virginia natural gas; inventory fears flip to shortage risk; huge new business driver.
Multi-faceted exposure: oil/gas benefiting from war, construction/infrastructure buildout, data-center power generation for hyperscaler gigawatt needs.
Integral to data center construction; trades at 36x earnings (like a tech stock) but valuation overheated despite good business.
